---
title: "XGBoost Polyglot T Demo"
format: html
filters:
  - tlang
---

# Introduction

This demo showcases a machine learning pipeline where training is done in **Python** (via XGBoost) and evaluation (confusion matrix) is done in **R** (via yardstick), all orchestrated by **T**.

# Evaluation Metrics

We can access the accuracy score calculated in Python.

```{t}
#| label: accuracy
accuracy = read_node("accuracy")
print(str_join(["Accuracy: ", str_string(accuracy * 100), "%"]))
```

# Model Performance

The T language manages the transfer of the prediction data from Python to R as an **Arrow** table.

```{t}
#| label: predictions
node_cm = inspect_node(read_node("confusion_matrix", as_node = true))
print(str_join(["Confusion matrix artifact path: ", node_cm.path]))
```

# Conclusion

T enables using the best tools for each stage of the ML lifecycle, such as XGBoost for performance and yardstick for elegant evaluation.
